questions.question: What is the primary purpose of having a business case for experience design?
options: B) To justify design decisions to stakeholders
questions.explanation: A business case provides a clear justification for design decisions, allowing stakeholders to understand the value of experience design and make informed decisions.

questions.question: What is a business case typically composed of?
questions.explanation: A business case typically includes user research and analysis, business goals and objectives, design concepts and prototypes, and other relevant factors to demonstrate the value of experience design.

questions.question: Why is it essential to have a business case for experience design?
options: C) To justify design decisions to stakeholders
questions.explanation: Having a business case for experience design helps to justify design decisions to stakeholders, improving the chances of getting buy-in and support for the design.

questions.question: What is a common outcome of a well-constructed business case for experience design?
questions.explanation: A well-constructed business case for experience design can lead to improved design decisions, as it provides a clear justification for design choices and helps stakeholders understand the value of experience design.

questions.question: Who typically benefits from a business case for experience design?
questions.explanation: Both designers and stakeholders benefit from a business case for experience design, as it helps designers make informed design decisions and stakeholders understand the value of experience design.
